title: Robust German Pork Stew
description: A hearty and sour German stew featuring pork, vinegar, and shio-koji cure, cooked to perfection in a claypot. This dish is kid-friendly and low-fat friendly.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 300 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large claypot, heat 2 tablespoons of oil over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it is translucent.
Add the pork cubes and cook until they are browned on all sides.
Add the shio-koji cure, salt, and black pepper, and stir to combine.
Add the apple cider vinegar, chicken broth, carrots, and potatoes, and stir to combine.
Cover the claypot with a lid and transfer it to the preheated oven.
Bake for 2 hours, or until the pork is tender and the vegetables are cooked through.
Remove the claypot from the oven and let it cool for 10 minutes.
Serve the stew hot, garnished with chopped fresh herbs if desired.
